content:
# Rus Formation

**Summary**: An Early Eocene carbonate formation.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] identified as a lower unit in the regional stratigraphy.

- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] modeled the Rus formation as a primary aquitard overlying the UER aquifer.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] part of the sedimentary sequence in the eastern part of Saudi Arabia.

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] studied the rus-formation to characterize its thickness and density.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] identified a collapsing structure within the Rus formation using resistivity data.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] modeled the Rus formation as a primary aquitard overlying the UER aquifer.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] part of the sedimentary sequence in the eastern part of Saudi Arabia.

- [[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018]] studied the rus-formation to characterize its thickness and density.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] identified a collapsing structure within the Rus formation using resistivity data.

## Reservoir Properties



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Not stated in source ([[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]])

- The Rus formation has an average thickness of 35 m in the study area and acts as an effective aquitard. ([[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012, p. 54]])

- The Rus Formation consists of thick marls and evaporites. ([[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018, p. 37]])

- The Rus Formation has a thickness of 50 m on the Dammam Dome outcrop. ([[2018-hanif|Hanif, 2018, p. 25]])

- Correlation of the resistivity model with the stratigraphic units displayed a probable boundary of a collapsing structure in the Rus formation. ([[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995, p. 28]])
